titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the George Wilson Pierson Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.
This collection contains 1 published digital object. And that object are comprised of 1 digital files.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday April 15, 2023 at 10:06

did.abstract: The papers consist of office files which contain notes on courses taken at Yale, teaching and faculty materials, manuscripts, typescripts, correspondence, subject and research files, and professional organizations materials. The bulk of the material relates to his work on the history of Yale and the National Endowment for the Humanities project.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Accession 1994-M-012 is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu requesting specific box numbers in order to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.
All other material is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by George Wilson Pierson has been transferred to Yale University. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact mssa.assist@yale.edu.

Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: George Wilson Pierson Papers. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of George W. Pierson, 1973, 1988, 1989, 1991, and 1993. Gift of the Estate of George W. Pierson, 1995. Gift of Radley H. Daly, 1990. Transferred from the Bibliography Department, 1988. Transferred from the John Farquhar Fulton Papers, 1996. Transferred from YRG 2-A-17, 1999.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: George Wilson Pierson (1904-1993) received his B.A. from Yale in 1926 and his Ph.D. in 1933. He became a member of the faculty of Yale University in 1926, holding the positions of professor of history, 1944-1946; Larned professor of history, 1946-1973; and emeritus since 1973. Pierson was also historian of Yale University and the author of many books. He married Mary Laetitia Pierson (1915-1982). He died on October 12, 1993.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of office files which contain notes on courses taken at Yale, teaching and faculty materials, manuscripts, typescripts, correspondence, subject and research files, and professional organizations materials. The bulk of the material relates to his work on the history of Yale and the National Endowment for the Humanities project.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Eliza D. Pierson Papers (MS 1428).

odd.head: General
odd.p: Box 36 includes files on the Corporation's Architectural Plan Committee, 1920-1931, and on the Alumni Committee on University Plan, 1917-1920. Box 37 includes files on The Commonwealth Fund, and on the Residential College Plan, 1910-1939.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The material is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email beinecke.library@yale.edu requesting specific box numbers in order to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.

appraisal.head: Appraisal
appraisal.p: Boxes 23, 24, 27-29, 44-46, 50-52 are no longer in collection.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This addition is composed of personal and family papers, including correspondence, diaries, memorabilia, legal and financial files, and photographs. The papers relate to George Pierson's parents and aunt, Eliza Day Pierson, who lived in Florida, New York. The papers also document George Pierson's education and travels in Europe.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This addition is organized in the order in which it was received from the donor.
